title: Immunohistochemistry using paraffin embedded tissue
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1mebt","text":"This protocol describes how to stain paraffin wax embedded sections of tissue for alpha-synuclein by immunofluorescence or DAB/peroxidase immunohistochemistry and is based on Hurley et al., 2013 (https://doi.org/10.1093/brain/awt134). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":196,"length":36}],"data":{}},{"key":"7339e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dqe38","text":"The protocol works for many other antibodies and the only change necessary for most antibodies is determining the optimal dilution of the primary antibody and the best type of antigen-retrieval to use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"18124E1BB69311ED9EF70A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://doi.org/10.1093/brain/awt134"}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"cg5k0","text":"Rabbit anti-alpha-synuclein    ab212184    Abcam","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1219e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9vf1i","text":"Goat anti-rabbit IgG biotin111-065-144-JIR  Stratech","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a9vho","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5iiap","text":"Goat anti-rabbit IgG Alexa Fluor™ 488 A27034 Thermo Fisher","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ffm09","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a0foi","text":"Streptavidin POD11089153001","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dissect tissue (fresh or trans-cardially perfused with formalin (10% neutral buffered formaldehyde) or 4% paraformaldehyde in PBS). Formaldehyde is toxic by inhalation HT501128 (sigmaaldrich.com)

Place tissue in at least 5 volumes of fixative at 4 °C for > 48:00:00 if tissue was perfused or > 96:00:00 if fresh. The tissue can stay in fixative indefinitely. If the tissue was fresh and the solution is turbid with blood, change the fixative after 24 hours.

Embed formalin fixed tissue in wax using an automated tissue processor. It can be done manually if you do not have access to an automated tissue processor. Histo-Clear™ II can be used instead of xylene. Xylene is toxic by inhalation and flammable Xylene - Safety Data Sheet (chemicalbook.com) Ethanol is toxic inhalation and flammable Ethanol - Safety Data Sheet (chemicalbook.com)
Wash with PBS 3 x 00:05:00
Incubate sections in biotinylated goat anti-rabbit IgG (1:250) secondary antibody for 2 hours.
Mount sections using Hydromount™ (National Diagnostics).
Incubate sections with streptavidin-peroxidase conjugate (1:250) for 1 hour.
Wash PBS 4 x 00:05:00
Visualise staining by incubation with PBS containing 0.05 % (w/v) 3,3′-Diaminobenzidine/0.015 % (v/v) H 2 O 2 /0.05 % (w/v) nickel ammonium sulphate for up to 00:03:00 ,
Counter stain with haematoxylin 00:00:05 to 00:00:10 if desired.
Wash sections with running tap water until it runs clear to remove excess stain.
Mount the tissue in a block of wax using an embedding station and metal or plastic moulds. Store at Room temperature
Section the tissue ( 5 µm to 8 µm )using a microtome. Store sections at Room temperature
Dewax sections in two changes of Histo-Clear II (National Diagnostics). 1. 00:10:00 2. 00:10:00
Rehydrate sections through graded (v/v) ethanol solutions. 1. 100 % 00:05:00 2. 100 % 00:05:00 3. 95 % 00:05:00 4. 80 % 00:05:00 5. 70 % 00:05:00
Wash with PBS 00:05:00
Wash with PBS 00:05:00
For antigen retrieval heat 100 °C the sections in 15 mM sodium citrate buffer containing 0.1% Tween® 20, pH 6 for 00:25:00 in an 800-watt microwave set on 30 % power.
Cool with running tap water 00:05:00
Draw around the sections with a wax pen.
Incubate with anti-alpha-synuclein antibody (1:500) (ab212184) in 10 % goat serum containing 0.005 % Triton™ X-100 and 0.05 % thimerosal (antibody buffer) 20:00:00 Room temperature
Wash with PBS 4 x 00:05:00
Wash with PBS 2 x 00:05:00
Heat sections 60 °C 00:30:00
Quench endogenous peroxidase with 0.3 % hydrogen peroxide (v/v) 00:05:00
Wash with PBS 2 x 00:05:00
incubated sections with Alexa Fluor goat anti-rabbit IgG (1:200) (A27034) 01:30:00
Dip slides in water to remove salts

Gut tissue 1. 10 % formalin 00:01:00 Room temperature 2. 10 % formalin 00:01:00 Room temperature 3. 70 % ethanol 01:00:00 30 °C 4. 90 % ethanol 01:00:00 30 °C 5. 100 % ethanol 01:00:00 30 °C 6. 100 % ethanol 01:00:00 30 °C 7. 100 % ethanol 01:00:00 30 °C 8. 100 % ethanol 01:20:00 30 °C 9. xylene 01:00:00 30 °C 10. xylene 01:00:00 30 °C 11. xylene 01:20:00 30 °C 12. paraffin wax 01:20:00 62 °C 13. paraffin wax 01:20:00 62 °C 14. paraffin wax 01:20:00 62 °C

Brain tissue (whole mouse) 1. 10 % formalin 00:01:00 Room temperature 2. 10% formalin 00:01:00 Room temperature 3. 70% ethanol 02:00:00 30 °C 4. 90% ethanol 02:00:00 30 °C 5. 100% ethanol 02:00:00 30 °C 6. 100% ethanol 02:00:00 30 °C 7. 100% ethanol 02:00:00 30 °C 8. 100% ethanol 02:00:00 30 °C 9. xylene 02:00:00 30 °C 10. xylene 02:00:00 30 °C 11. xylene 04:00:00 30 °C 12. paraffin wax 02:00:00 62 °C 13. paraffin wax 02:00:00 62 °C 14. paraffin wax 02:00:00 62 °C
Select slides to be stained and place in a microscope slide rack.
incubate sections with DAPI (1 µg/ml) 00:05:00
For immunofluorescence staining go to step 17 For DAB/peroxidase staining go to step 27

Block non-specific binding sites with 10 % goat serum in PBS containing 0.005 % Triton™ X-100 and 0.05 % thimerosal 01:30:00 Depending on the size and number of sections 150-250 microliters of solution should be sufficient to cover the sections.
Clear sections with Histo-Clear II (National Diagnostics) 2 x 00:05:00
Dehydrate sections through graded (v/v) ethanol solutions 70 % 00:05:00 80 % 00:05:00 95 % 00:05:00 100 % 00:05:00 100 % 00:05:00
Mount with Omnimount™ (National Diagnostics) or equivalent permanent resin.
Wash sections for > 00:05:00 in running tap water.
Wash PBS 4 x 00:05:00
Incubate with anti-alpha-synuclein antibody (1:500) (ab212184) in 10 % goat serum containing 0.005 % Triton™ X-100 and 0.05 % thimerosal (antibody buffer) 20:00:00 Room temperature
Wash PBS 4 x 00:05:00
Draw around the sections with a wax pen.

Block non-specific binding sites with 10 % goat serum in PBS containing 0.005 % Triton™ X-100 and 0.05 % thimerosal 01:30:00 Depending on the size and number of sections 150-250 microliters of solution should be sufficient to cover the sections.
creator: Michael J Hurley
